title: Ranunculus pseudocassubicus
intro: Ranunculus pseudocassubicus is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. Bioactive constituents of R. pseudocassubicus include ranunculin, protoanemonin, and other glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with R. pseudocassubicus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Ranunculus pseudocassubicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is commonly known as the false cassubic Ranunculus. It is native to Europe, specifically found in the mountainous regions. Ranunculus pseudocassubicus is classified as a perennial herb, growing up to a certain height. The plant belongs to the Ranunculus genus, which consists of over 600 species of buttercups and their relatives.
